name: Bentonite Clay
description: A naturally occurring montmorillonite mineral clay with a powerful negative ionic charge, capable of binding positively-charged toxins, heavy metals, and bacteria.
chemicalName: Hydrated sodium calcium aluminium magnesium silicate hydroxide
appearance: Fine grey-green to cream powder; smooth, slightly slippery texture when wet
solubility: Insoluble in water; swells to absorb water up to several times its dry volume
chemistry: Montmorillonite mineral with strong negative ionic charge. Swells in water, creating electrical attraction that binds positively-charged toxins, heavy metals, and bacteria. Rich in calcium, magnesium, silica, iron. Two types: sodium bentonite (higher swelling) and calcium bentonite (preferred for oral care due to calcium content).
warnings: Never contact metal — loses ionic charge. Use wooden/plastic utensils. Store in glass.
